data.body:
REPORT-FIDELITY CHECK (real acli, project DIG) — answers "does cap-0 still produce a useful report of what a writing mode would do?":

YES, at the differ/plan level (verified):
- Empty local store + DIG (993 issues already rebar-id-labeled) -> outbound_differ total=0, inbound_differ total=0: correctly "in sync, nothing to reconcile".
- Added ONE local-only ticket (no Jira counterpart) -> outbound_differ total=1 create=1: exactly the outbound-create a writing mode would attempt.
cap-0 runs the SAME differ computation a writing mode uses; differ-level dedup (rebar-id label stand-down) IS reflected. So the report faithfully shows "what is out of sync / what would be attempted".

CAVEAT (existing cap-0 semantics, NOT changed by the no-write fix): cap-0 defers all mutations and invokes NO applier leaves, so APPLY-TIME resolution is not exercised — notably outbound creates that would dedup-skip at apply time (matching Jira issue found by summary) still appear as "create" in the report. So the report can slightly OVERSTATE vs the final applied count. It is a discrepancy/plan report, not a guaranteed apply-outcome.

IMPLICATION FOR THE FIX: the high-level report (RECON outbound/inbound differ totals) is already returned to callers via stderr. The DETAILED per-mutation plan is currently written to a bridge_state manifest FILE — which the no-write fix must NOT write. So the fix should RETURN the detailed plan in the result dict instead of persisting it (the data is all in-memory). Net: the no-write change does not reduce report usefulness; it motivates surfacing the report in the return value rather than as a side-effect file.
